title: Coryphantha robbinsiorum
intro: Coryphantha robbinsiorum, also known as Robbins' Pincushion Cactus, is a species of cactus in the genus Coryphantha, characterized by its globular stems and short, fine spines. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Coryphantha robbinsiorum include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Coryphantha robbinsiorum is often used to make decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to have beneficial effects on the immune system and can be used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Coryphantha robbinsiorum is classified within the family Cactaceae, specifically in the subfamily Cactoideae. This species belongs to the genus Coryphantha, which comprises several species of small to medium-sized cacti. Within the genus, Coryphantha robbinsiorum is part of a group known as the "sulcatas" due to its characteristic sulcate stem. The species is native to the United States and Mexico. Its classification is further supported by its unique morphological features, such as its stem and flower structure.
